rationale: The citing paper uses Meissner et al. (2016) to support its core framing that repeated elicitation tasks induce adaptive decision processes. It cites this paper when motivating conjoint process-tracing evidence and explicitly when stating that H1 and H2 are likely true in sufficiently long tasks, which feeds into its formal model and hypothesis setup. This is more than a passing background list because the citation helps justify key theoretical premises of the paper. However, the citing study does not directly reuse Meissner et al.'s data or method as its main empirical base.
relation_description: Meissner et al. (2016) provides prior evidence on adaptation and increasing efficiency in repeated conjoint choices, which the citing paper treats as a conceptual anchor for its adaptation-based hypotheses. The citing paper then builds its own argument about how such adaptation can reduce external validity as more elicitation questions are asked.
